19.28: Jesus said to them, Truly I say to you, that in the new world when the Son of man shall sit on the throne of his glory, you who have come after me shall also sit on twelve chairs, and you shall judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
19.29: And every man who leaves houses, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or fields, for my name’s sake, shall receive a hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life.
19.30: But many who are first shall be last, and the last first.

20.1: FOR the kingdom of heaven is like a man, who is a householder, who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
20.2: He bargained with the laborers for a penny a day, and sent them to his vineyard.
20.3: And he went out at the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the market place.
20.4: And he said to them, You also go to the vineyard, and I will give you what is right. And they went.
20.5: And he went out again at the sixth and at the ninth hour, and did the same.
20.6: And towards the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing idle, and he said to them, Why do you stand all day idle?
20.7: They said to him, Because no man has hired us. He said to them, You also go to the vineyard, and you will receive what is right.
20.8: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his steward, Call the laborers and pay them their wages; and begin from the last ones to the first.
20.9: When those of the eleventh hour came, they each received a penny.
20.10: But when the first ones came, they expected to receive more; but they also got each one a penny.
20.11: And when they received it, they murmured against the householder,
20.12: Saying, These last ones have worked only one hour, and you have made them equal with us who have borne the weight of the day and its heat.
20.13: He answered and said to one of them, My friend, I am not doing you an injustice; did you not bargain with me for a penny?
20.14: Take what is yours and go away; I wish to give to this last one the same as to you.
20.15: Have I no right to do what I wish with mine own? Or are you jealous because I am generous?
20.16: Even so the last shall be first, and the first last; for many are called, but few are chosen.
20.17: Now Jesus was ready to go up to Jerusalem; and he took his twelve disciples apart on the road, and he said to them,
20.18: Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man will be delivered to the high priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death.
20.19: And they will deliver him to the Gentiles and they will mock him, and scourge him, and crucify him; and on the third day he will rise up.
20.20: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came up to him, together with her sons; and she worshipped him, and requested something of him.
20.21: He said to her, What do you wish? She said to him, Command that these two sons of mine sit, one at your right and one at your left, in your kingdom.
20.22: Jesus answered and said, You do not know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup that I am ready to drink, or be baptized with the baptism with which I am to be baptized? They said to him, We can.
20.23: He said to them, Indeed my cup you shall drink, and the baptism with which I am to be baptized, you too shall be baptized with; but to sit at my right hand and at my left, that is not mine to give, but it is for those for whom it is prepared by my Father.
20.24: When the ten heard it, they were angry at the two brothers.
20.25: And Jesus called them and said, You know that the princes of the people are also their owners; and their officials rule over them.
20.26: Let not this be so among you; but whoever wishes to be great among you, let him be a minister to you;
20.27: And whoever wishes to be first among you, let him be a servant to you;
20.28: Just as the Son of man did not come to be ministered to, but to minister, and to give his life as a salvation for the sake of many.
20.29: And when Jesus went out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
20.30: And behold, two blind men were sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried aloud, saying, Have mercy upon us, O Lord, son of David.
20.31: But the people rebuked them to keep quiet; but they cried louder, saying, Our Lord, have mercy upon us, son of David.
20.32: And Jesus stopped and called them, and he said, What do you wish me to do for you?
20.33: They said to him, Our Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
20.34: And Jesus had mercy upon them, so he touched their eyes; and immediately their eyes were opened, and they followed him.

21.1: WHEN he came near to Jerusalem, he came to Bethphage on the side of the Mount of Olives. Jesus then sent two of his disciples,
21.2: And he said to them, Go to that village which is in front of you, and straightway you will find an ass which is tied up, and a colt with her; untie them and bring them to me.
21.3: And if any man should say anything to you, tell him that our Lord needs them; and he will immediately send them here.
21.4: All this happened, so that what was said by the prophet, might be fulfilled, who said,
21.5: Tell the daughter of Zion, Behold your king is coming to you, meek, and riding upon an ass, and upon a colt, the foal of an ass.
21.6: And the disciples went and did as Jesus had commanded them.
21.7: And they brought the ass and the colt, and they put their garments on the colt, and Jesus rode on it.
21.8: And a great many people spread their garments on the road; and others cut down branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
21.9: And the people who were going before him and coming after him, were shouting and saying, Hosanna to the son of David; Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest.
21.10: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up, and they were saying, Who is this man?
21.11: And the people were saying. This is the prophet, Jesus, from Nazareth in Galilee.
21.12: And Jesus entered into the temple of God, and put out all who were buying and selling in the temple, and he overturned the trays of the money-changers and the stands of those who sold doves.
21.13: And he said to them, It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but you have made it a bandits’ cave.
21.14: And in the temple they brought to him the blind and the lame, and he healed them.
21.15: But when the high priests and the Pharisees saw the wonders that he did, and the boys who were crying aloud in the temple, and saying, Hosanna to the son of David, they were displeased.
21.16: And they said to him, Do you hear what they are saying? Jesus said to them, Yes; have you never read, From the mouth of little children and of boys you made praise?
21.17: And he left them, and went outside of the city to Bethany, and he lodged there.
21.18: In the morning, as he returned to the city, he became hungry.
21.19: And he saw a fig tree on the roadside, and he came to it and found nothing on it except leaves; and he said to it, Let there be no fruit on you again for ever. And shortly the fig tree withered.
21.20: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed and said, How is it that the fig tree has withered so soon?
21.21: Jesus answered and said to them, Truly I say to you, If you have faith, and do not doubt, you will perform a deed not only like this of the fig tree, but should you say even to this mountain, Be removed and fall into the sea, it shall be done.
21.22: And everything that you will ask in prayer and believe, you shall receive.
21.23: When Jesus came to the temple, the high priests and the elders of the people came up to him, while he was teaching, and said to him, By what authority do you do these things? and who gave you this authority?
21.24: Jesus answered and said to them, I will also ask you a word, and if you tell me, I will then tell you by what authority I do these things.
21.25: Whence is the baptism of John? Is it from heaven, or from men? And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we should say from heaven, he will say to us, Why then did you not believe him?
21.26: And if we should say, from men, we are afraid of the people, for all of them regard John as a prophet.
21.27: So they answered and said to him, We do not know. Jesus said to them, Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
21.28: What do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first one and said to him, My son, go and work today in the vineyard.
21.29: He answered and said, I do not want to, but later he regretted and went.
21.30: And he came to the other one and said to him likewise. And he answered and said, Here am I, my Lord, and yet he did not go.
21.31: Which of these two did the will of his father? They said to him, The first one. Jesus said to them, Truly I say to you, that even the publicans and the harlots will precede you into the kingdom of God.
21.32: For John came to you in a righteousness way, and you did not believe him; but the publicans and the harlots believed him; but you, even though you saw, did not repent, so that later you may believe him.
21.33: Hear another parable. There was a man who was a householder, and he planted a vineyard, and fenced it, and he dug in it a winepress, and built a tower, and then he leased it to laborers, and went away on a journey.
21.34: And when the fruit season was at hand, he sent his servants to the laborers, that they might send him of the fruits of his vineyard.
21.35: And the laborers seized his servants, and some were beaten, and some were stoned, and some were killed.
21.36: Again he sent other servants, many more than the first; and they did likewise to them.
21.37: At last he sent his son to them, saying, They might feel ashamed before my son.
21.38: But when the laborers saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him and retain his inheritance.
21.39: So they seized him, and took him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
21.40: When therefore the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those laborers?
21.41: They said to him, He will destroy them severely, and lease his vineyard to other laborers, who will give him fruits in their seasons.
21.42: Jesus said to them, Have you never read in the scripture, The stone which the builders rejected, the same became the cornerstone; this was from the Lord, and it is a marvel in our eyes?
21.43: Therefore I say to you, that the kingdom of God will be taken away from you, and will be given to a people who bear fruits.
21.44: And whoever falls on this stone will be broken, and on whomever it falls it will scatter him.
21.45: When the high priests and Pharisees heard his parables, they understood that he was speaking against them.
21.46: So they wanted to arrest him, but they were afraid of the people, because they regarded him as a prophet.

22.1: AND Jesus answered again by parables, and said,
22.2: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who gave a marriage-feast for his son.
22.3: And he sent his servants to call those who were invited to the marriage-feast, but they would not come.
22.4: Again he sent other servants and said, Tell those who are invited, Behold my supper is ready, and my oxen and fatlings are killed, and everything is prepared; come to the marriage- feast.
22.5: But they sneered at it, and went away, one to his field, another to his business;
22.6: And the rest seized his servants and insulted them, and killed them.
22.7: When the king heard it he was angry; and he sent out his armies and destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
22.8: Then he said to his servants, Now the marriage-feast is ready, and those who were invited were unworthy.
22.9: Go, therefore, to the main roads, and whomever you may find, invite them to the marriage- feast.
22.10: So the servants went out to the roads and gathered together every one they could find, bad and good; and the wedding-house was filled with guests.
22.11: When the king entered to see the guests, he saw there a man who was not wearing wedding garments.
22.12: And he said to him, My friend, how did you enter here, when you do not have wedding garments? And he was speechless.
22.13: Then the king said to the servants, Bind his hands and his feet and take him out into darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
22.14: For many are invited, and few are chosen.
22.15: Then the Pharisees went away and took counsel how to trap him by a word.
22.16: So they sent to him their disciples together with the Herodians, and they said to him, Teacher, we know that you are true, and you teach the way of God justly; and you do not favor any man, for you do not discriminate between men.
22.17: Tell us, therefore, what do you think? Is it lawful to pay head-tax to Caesar, or not?
22.18: But Jesus knew their evil, and said, Why do you tempt me, O hypocrites?
22.19: Show me the head-tax penny. And they brought to him a penny.
22.20: And Jesus said to them, Whose is this image and inscription?
22.21: They said, Caesar’s. He said to them, Give therefore to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.
22.22: And when they heard it, they were amazed; and they left him and went away.
22.23: That same day the Sadducees came and said to him, There is no resurrection of the dead; and they asked him,
22.24: And said to him, Teacher, Moses has told us, If a man die without sons, let his brother take his wife, and raise up offspring for his brother.
22.25: Now there were with us seven brothers; the first married and died, and because he had no sons, he left his wife to his brother.
22.26: Likewise the second, also the third, up to the seventh.
22.27: And after them all the woman also died.
22.28: Therefore at the resurrection, to which of these seven will she be a wife? for they all married her.
22.29: Jesus answered, and said to them, You err, because you do not understand the scriptures nor the power of God.
22.30: For at the resurrection of the dead, they neither marry women, nor are women given to men in marriage, but they are like the angels of God in heaven.
22.31: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God, saying,
22.32: I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, the God of Jacob? And yet God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
22.33: And when the people heard it, they were amazed at his teaching.
22.34: But when the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
22.35: And one of them who knew the law, asked him, testing him,
22.36: Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the law?
22.37: Jesus said to him, Love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your power, and with all your mind.
22.38: This is the greatest and the first commandment.
22.39: And the second is like to it, Love your neighbor as yourself.
22.40: On these two commandments hang the law and the prophets.
22.41: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
22.42: And he said, What do you say concerning the Christ? whose son is he? They said to him, son of David.
22.43: He said to them, How is it then that David through the Spirit calls him Lord? For he said,
22.44: The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.
22.45: If David then calls him Lord, how can he be his son?
22.46: And no man was able to answer him, and from that day no man dared to question him.

23.1: THEN Jesus spoke with the people and with his disciples.
23.2: And he said to them, The scribes and the Pharisees sit on the chair of Moses.
23.3: Therefore whatever they tell you to obey, obey and do it, but do not do according to their works; for they say and do not.
23.4: And they bind heavy burdens, and put them on men’s shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to touch them, even with their finger.
23.5: And all their works they do, just to be seen by men; for they widen the fringes of their garments, and they lengthen the ends of their robes,
23.6: And they like the chief places at feasts, and the front seats in the synagogues,
23.7: And the greetings in the streets, and to be called by men, Rabbi.
23.8: But you do not be called, Rabbi; for one is your Master, and all you are brethren.
23.9: And call no one on earth, father, for one is your Father in heaven.
23.10: Nor be called leaders, for one is your leader, the Christ.
23.11: But he who is greatest among you, let him be your minister.
23.12: For whoever exalts himself shall be humbled; and whoever humbles himself shall be exalted.
23.13: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you embezzle the property of widows, with the pretense that you make long prayers; because of this you shall receive a greater judgment.
23.14: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you have shut off the kingdom of heaven against men; for you do not enter into it yourselves, and do not permit those who would enter.
23.15: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you traverse sea and land to make one proselyte; and when he becomes one, you make him the son of hell twice more than yourselves.
23.16: Woe to you, blind guides, for you say, Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold which is in the temple, he is guilty!
23.17: O you fools and blind! for which is greater, the gold or the temple that sanctifies the gold?
23.18: And whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the offering that is on it, he is guilty.
23.19: O you fools and blind! for which is greater, the offering, or the altar that sanctifies the offering?
23.20: Therefore he who swears by the altar, he swears by it and by everything that is on it.
23.21: And whoever swears by the temple, swears by it and by him who dwells in it.
23.22: And he who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God, and by him who sits on it.
23.23: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you take tithes on mint, dill, and cummin, and you have overlooked the more important matters of the law, such as justice, mercy, and trustworthiness. These were necessary for you to have done, and the same by no means to have left undone.
23.24: O blind guides, who strain at gnats and swallow camels!
23.25: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! you clean the outside of the cup and of the dish, but inside they are full of extortion and iniquity.
23.26: Blind Pharisees! clean first the inside of the cup and of the dish, so that their outside may also be clean.
23.27: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you are like tombs painted white, which look beautiful from the outside, but inside are full of dead bones and all kinds of corruption.
23.28: Even so, from the outside you appear to men to be righteous, but from within you are full of iniquity and hypocrisy.
23.29: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you build the tombs of the prophets, and you decorate the graves of the righteous;
23.30: And you say, If we had been living in the days of our forefathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.
23.31: Now you testify concerning yourselves, that you are the children of those who killed the prophets.
23.32: You also fill up the measure of your fathers.
23.33: O you serpents, and seed of scorpions! how can you flee from the judgment of hell?
23.34: Because of this, I am sending to you prophets and wise men and scribes; some of them you will kill and crucify; and some you will scourge in your synagogues, and pursue them from city to city;
23.35: So that all the blood of the righteous shed on the ground may come on you, from the blood of Abel the righteous down to the blood of Zachariah, son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
23.36: Truly I say to you, All of these things shall come upon this generation.
23.37: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, murderess of the prophets, and stoner of those who are sent to her! how often I wanted to gather together your children, just as a hen gathers her chickens under her wings, and yet you would not!
23.38: Behold, your house will be left to you desolate.
23.39: For I say to you, from now you will not see me until you say, Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.

24.1: AND Jesus went out of the temple to go away; and his disciples came up to him, and were showing him the building of the temple.
24.2: But he said to them, Behold, do you not see all of these? truly I say to you, Not a stone shall be left here upon a stone, which will not be torn down.
24.3: While Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came up talking among themselves, and they said to him, Tell us when these things will happen, and what is the sign of your coming, and of the end of the world?
24.4: Jesus answered and said to them, Be careful that no man deceives you.
24.5: For many will come in my name, and say, I am the Christ, and they will deceive many.
24.6: You are bound to hear of revolutions and rumors of wars; look out and do not be disturbed; for all of these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
24.7: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there will be famines and plagues and earthquakes, in different places.
24.8: But all these things are just the beginning of travail.
24.9: Then they will deliver you over to be oppressed, and they will kill you; and you will be hated by all nations for my name’s sake.
24.10: Then many will stumble, and they will hate one another, and betray one another.
24.11: And many false prophets will rise, and will mislead a great many.
24.12: And because of the growth of iniquity, the love of many will become cold.
24.13: But he who has patience to the end, he will be saved.
24.14: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations; then the end will come.
24.15: When you see the sign of the refuse of desolation, as spoken by the prophet Daniel, accumulating in the holy place, whoever reads will understand it.
24.16: Then let those who are in Judaea, flee to the mountain,
24.17: And he who is on the roof, let him not come down to take things out of his house.
24.18: And he who is in the field, let him not return back to take his clothes.
24.19: But woe to those who are with child, and to those who give suck in those days!
24.20: Pray that your flight may not be in winter, nor on the sabbath.
24.21: For then will be great suffering, such as has never happened from the beginning of the world until now, and never will be again.
24.22: And if those days were not shortened, no flesh would live; but for the sake of the chosen ones those days will be shortened.
24.23: Then if any man should say to you, Behold, here is the Christ, or there, do not believe it.
24.24: For there will rise false Christs and lying prophets, and they will show signs and great wonders, so as to mislead, if possible, even the chosen ones.
24.25: Behold, I have told you in advance.
24.26: Therefore, if they should say to you, Behold, he is in the desert, do not go out; or, behold, he is in the room, do not believe it.
24.27: For just as the lightning comes out from the east, and is seen even in the west, so will be the coming of the Son of man.
24.28: For wherever the corpse is, there will the eagles gather.
24.29: Immediately after the suffering of those days, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give her light, and the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the universe will be shaken.
24.30: Then the sign of the Son of man will appear in the sky; and then all the generations of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of man coming on the clouds of the sky, with an army and great glory.
24.31: And he will send his angels with a large trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four winds, from one end of the universe to the other.
24.32: From the fig tree learn a parable. As soon as its branches become tender and bring forth leaves, you know that summer is coming.
24.33: So even you, when you see all these things, know that it has arrived at the door.
24.34: Truly I say to you, that this generation will not pass away, until all these things happen.
24.35: Even heaven and earth will pass away, but my words shall not pass away.
24.36: But concerning that day and that hour, no man knows, not even the angels of heaven, but the Father alone.
24.37: Just as in the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of man.
24.38: For as the people before the flood were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered into the ark,
24.39: And they knew nothing until the flood came and carried them all away; such will be the coming of the Son of man.
24.40: Then two men will be in the field, one will be taken away and the other left.
24.41: Two women will be grinding at the handmill, one will be taken and the other left.
24.42: Be alert, therefore, for you do not know at what hour your Lord will come.
24.43: But know this much, that if the master of the house knew at what watch of the night the thief comes, he would keep awake and would not let his house be plundered.
24.44: For this reason, you also be ready, for the Son of man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.
24.45: Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his Lord has appointed over his household, to give them food in due time?
24.46: Blessed is that servant, when his Lord comes and finds him so doing.
24.47: Truly I say to you, he will appoint him over all that he has.
24.48: But if a bad servant should say in his heart, My lord will delay his coming,
24.49: And he begins to beat his fellow servants, and to eat and drink with drunkards,
24.50: The Lord of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect, and at an hour that he does not know.
24.51: And he will severely scourge him, and give him a portion like that of the hypocrites; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

25.1: THEN the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins, who took their lamps, and went out to greet the bridegroom and the bride.
25.2: Five of them were wise, and five were foolish.
25.3: And the foolish ones took their lamps, but took no oil with them.
25.4: But the wise ones took oil in the vessels with their lamps.
25.5: As the bridegroom was delayed, they all slumbered and slept.
25.6: And at midnight there was a cry, Behold, the bridegroom is coming; go out to greet him.
25.7: Then all the virgins got up and fixed their lamps.
25.8: And the foolish ones said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.
25.9: Then the wise ones answered and said, Why, there would not be enough for us and for you; go to those who sell and buy for yourselves.
25.10: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and those who were ready entered with him into the wedding house, and the door was locked.
25.11: Afterward the other virgins also came and said, Our lord, our lord, open to us.
25.12: But he answered and said to them, Truly I say to you, I do not know you.
25.13: Be alert, therefore, for you do not know, that day nor the hour.
25.14: It is just like a man who went on a journey, who called his servants and put his wealth in their charge.
25.15: To one he gave five talents, to one two, to another one; to each one according to his ability; and immediately he went on a journey.
25.16: The one who received five talents then went and traded with them, and he earned five others.
25.17: Likewise the second one, he gained by trading gained two others.
25.18: But he who received one, went and dug in the ground, and hid his lord’s money.
25.19: After a long time, the lord of those servants returned, and took an accounting from them.
25.20: Then the one who received five talents came up, and offered five others, and he said, My lord, you gave me five talents; behold, I have gained five others to them.
25.21: His lord said to him, Well done, good and reliable servant; you have been faithful over a little, I will appoint you over much; enter into your master’s joy.
25.22: Then the one with the two talents came and he said, My lord, you gave me two talents, behold I have gained two others to them.
25.23: His lord said to him, Well done, good and reliable servant, you have been faithful over a little, I will appoint you over much; enter into your master’s joy.
25.24: Then the one who received one talent also came up, and he said, My lord, I knew that you are a hard man, and you reap where you did not sow, and gather where you did not scatter.
25.25: So I was afraid, and I went and hid your talent in the ground; here it is, it is your own one.
25.26: His lord answered and said to him, O wicked and lazy servant, you knew me that I reap where I did not sow, and I gather where I did not scatter.
25.27: You should then have put my money in the exchange, and when I returned I would have demanded my own with interest.
25.28: Therefore take away the talent from him, and give it to the one who has ten talents.
25.29: For to him who has, it shall he given, and it shall increase to him; but he who has not, even that which he has shall be taken away from him.
25.30: And the idle servant they threw into the outer darkness; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
25.31: When the Son of man comes in his glory, and all his holy angels with him, then he will sit upon the throne of his glory.
25.32: And all nations will gather before him; and he will separate them one from another, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats;
25.33: And he will set the sheep at his right, and the goats at his left.
25.34: Then the King will say to those at his right, Come, you blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom which has been prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
25.35: For I was hungry, and you gave me to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave me to drink; I was a stranger and you took me in;
25.36: I was naked, and you covered me; I was sick, and you visited me; I was in prison, and you came to me.
25.37: Then the righteous will say to him, Our Lord, when did we see you hungry, and feed you? or thirsty and gave you drink?
25.38: And when did we see you a stranger, and took you in? Or that you were naked, and covered you?
25.39: And when did we see you sick, or in the prison, and come to you?
25.40: The king then will answer and say to them, Truly I tell you, Inasmuch as you have done it to one of these least brethren, you did it to me.
25.41: Then he will also say to those at his left, Go away from me, you cursed, to the everlasting fire, which is prepared for the adversary and his angels.
25.42: For I was hungry, and you did not give me to eat; I was thirsty, and you did not give me to drink;
25.43: I was a stranger, and you did not take me in; I was naked, and you did not cover me; I was sick and in prison and you did not visit me.
25.44: Then they also will answer and say, Our lord, when did we see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick or in the prison, and did not minister to you?
25.45: Then he will answer and say to them, Truly I say to you, Inasmuch as you did not do it to one of these least ones, you also did not do it to me.
25.46: And these shall go into everlasting torment, and the righteous into eternal life.

26.1: WHEN Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said to his disciples,
26.2: You know that after two days will be the passover, and the Son of man will be betrayed to be crucified.
26.3: Then the high priests and the scribes and the elders of the people assembled in the court yard of the high priest, who is called Caiaphas.
26.4: And they took counsel concerning Jesus, to arrest him by a snare and kill him.
26.5: And they said, Not on the feast day, so as not to cause a riot among the people.
26.6: And when Jesus was at Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
26.7: A woman came up to him with an alabaster vessel of precious perfume, and she poured it upon the head of Jesus, while he was reclining.
26.8: When his disciples saw it, they were dis- pleased, and said, Why is this loss?
26.9: For it could have been sold for a great deal, and given to the poor.
26.10: But Jesus understood it and said to them, Why are you troubling the woman? She has done a good work to me.
26.11: For you always have the poor with you, but you will not have me always.
26.12: But this one who poured the perfume on my body, did it as for my burial.
26.13: And truly I say to you, Wherever this my gospel is preached throughout the world, what she has done will also be told as a memorial to her.
26.14: Then one of the twelve, called Judas of Iscariot, went to the high priests;
26.15: And he said to them, What are you willing to give me, and I will deliver him to you? And they promised him thirty pieces of silver.
26.16: And from that time he sought an opportunity to betray him.
26.17: On the first day of unleavened bread, the disciples came up to Jesus and said to him, Where do you wish that we may prepare the passover for you to eat?
26.18: And he said to them, Go into the city to a certain man, and say to him, Our Master says, My time has come, I will observe the passover with my disciples at your house.
26.19: And his disciples did as Jesus had commanded them; and they prepared the passover.
26.20: And when it was evening, he was reclining with his twelve disciples.
26.21: And while they were eating he said, Truly I say to you, that one of you will betray me.
26.22: And they felt very sad, and began to say to him one by one, Why, is it I, my Lord?
26.23: And he answered and said, He who dips his hand with me in the dish, he will betray me.
26.24: The Son of man is going through, just as it is written concerning him; but woe to the man by whose hand the Son of man is betrayed! it would have been far better for that man never to have been born.
26.25: Then Judas the traitor answered and said, Master, perhaps it is I? Jesus said to him, You say that.
26.26: While they were eating, Jesus took bread and blessed it, and he broke it, and gave it to his disciples, and he said, Take, eat; this is my body.
26.27: Then he took the cup and gave thanks, and gave it to them and said, Take, drink of it, all of you.
26.28: This is my blood of the new covenant which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
26.29: But I say to you, from now on I shall not drink from this fruit of the vine, until the day when I drink it anew with you in the kingdom of God.
26.30: And they offered praise, and went out to the Mount of Olives.
26.31: Then Jesus said to them, All of you will deny me this night; for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of his flock will be scattered.
26.32: But after I am risen, I will be in Galilee before you.
26.33: Peter answered and said to him, Even if every man should deny you, I will never deny you.
26.34: Jesus said to him, Truly I say to you, that in this very night, before the cock crows, you will deny me three times.
26.35: Peter said to him, Even if I must die with you, I will never deny you. All the disciples said likewise.
26.36: Then Jesus came with them to a place which is called Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, Sit down here, while I go to pray.
26.37: And he took Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and he began to be sorrowful and oppressed.
26.38: He said to them, My soul is sorrowful even to death; wait for me here, and watch with me.
26.39: And he went a little further and fell on his face, and prayed saying, O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me; but let it be, not as I will, but as you.
26.40: Then he came to his disciples and found them sleeping, and he said to Peter, So, you were not able to watch with me even for one hour?
26.41: Awake and pray, that you may not enter into temptation; the spirit indeed is ready, but the body is weak.
26.42: He went away again the second time and prayed and said, O my Father, if this cup cannot pass, and if I have to drink it, let it be according to your will.
26.43: He came again and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.
26.44: And he left them and went away again and prayed the third time, and he said the same word.
26.45: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Sleep from now on and get your rest; behold, the hour has come, and the Son of man will be delivered into the hands of sinners.
26.46: Arise, let us go; behold, he who is to deliver me has arrived.
26.47: While he was speaking, behold, Judas the traitor, one of the twelve, came and with him a large crowd with swords and staves, from the high priests and the elders of the people.
26.48: Now Judas the traitor had given them a sign, saying, He whom I kiss, it is he, arrest him.
26.49: And immediately he came up to Jesus and said, Peace, Master; and he kissed him.
26.50: Jesus said to him, Is it for this that you have come, my friend? Then they came near and laid hands on Jesus, and arrested him.
26.51: And behold, one of those who were with Jesus stretched out his hand and drew a sword, and struck it at the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
26.52: Then Jesus said to him, Return the sword to its place; for all who take swords will die by swords.
26.53: Or do you think that I cannot ask of my Father, and he will now raise up for me more than twelve legions of angels?
26.54: How then could the scriptures be fulfilled, that it must be so?
26.55: At that very hour Jesus said to the people, Have you come out with swords and staves to arrest me like a bandit? I sat with you every day, teaching in the temple, and you did not arrest me.
26.56: But this has happened so that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left him, and fled.
26.57: And those who had arrested Jesus took him to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders had assembled.
26.58: But Simon Peter followed him afar off, up to the courtyard of the high priest, and he went inside and sat with the soldiers, to see the end.
26.59: Now the high priests and the elders and the whole council were seeking witnesses against Jesus, so that they might put him to death.
26.60: But they could not find any; then there came a great many false witnesses; but at the end two came forward,
26.61: And said, This man said, I can tear down the temple of God, and build it in three days.
26.62: And the high priest stood up and said to him, You are not answering anything. What is it that these men testify against you?
26.63: But Jesus was silent. Then the high priest answered and said to him, I adjure you by the living God, to tell us if you are the Christ, the Son of God?
26.64: Jesus said to him, You say that. But I say to you that from henceforth you will see the Son of man sitting at the right hand of the power, and coming upon the clouds of the sky.
26.65: The high priest then rent his clothes and said, Behold, he is blaspheming; why therefore do we need witnesses? behold, you have now heard his blasphemy.
26.66: What else do you want? They answered and said, He is guilty of death.
26.67: Then they spat in his face, and struck him on his head, and others beat him,
26.68: Saying, O Christ, prophesy to us; who smote you?
26.69: Now Peter sat outside in the courtyard; and a maidservant came up to him, and said to him, You also were with Jesus the Nazarene.
26.70: But he denied it before all of them, and said, I do not understand what you are saying.
26.71: And as he was going to the porch, another one saw him, and she said to them, This man was also there with Jesus the Nazarene.
26.72: Again he denied it with oaths, I do not know the man.
26.73: After a while, those who were standing came up, and said to Peter, Truly you also are one of them, for even your speech proves it.
26.74: Then he began to curse and to swear, I do not know the man. At that very hour the cock crowed.
26.75: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus, which he had said to him, Before the cock crows, you will deny me three times. And he went outside and wept bitterly.

27.1: WHEN it was morning, the high priests and the elders of the people took counsel concerning Jesus, how to put him to death.
27.2: So they bound him, and took him and delivered him to Pilate the governor.
27.3: Then Judas the traitor, when he saw that Jesus was convicted, repented, and went away and brought back the same thirty pieces of silver to the high priests and the elders.
27.4: And he said, I have sinned, because I have betrayed innocent blood. But they said to him, What is that to us? You know better.
27.5: Then he threw the silver in the temple, and departed; and he went and hanged himself.
27.6: The high priests took the silver and said, It is not lawful to put it in the house of offerings, because it is the price of blood.
27.7: And they took counsel, and bought with it the potter’s field, for a cemetery for strangers.
27.8: On this account that field was called The field of blood, to this day.
27.9: Then what was spoken by the prophet was fulfilled, who said, I took the thirty pieces of silver, the costly price which was bargained with the children of Israel.
27.10: And I gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord commanded me.
27.11: And Jesus stood before the governor; and the governor asked him and said to him, Are you the King of the Jews? Jesus said to him, You say that.
27.12: And while the chief priests and elders were accusing him, he gave no answer.
27.13: Then Pilate said to him, Do you not hear how much they testify against you?
27.14: But he did not answer him, not even a word; and because of this Pilate marvelled greatly.
27.15: Now on every feast day it was the custom of the governor to release one prisoner to the people, anyone whom they wanted.
27.16: They had a well-known prisoner, called Bar-Abbas, who was bound.
27.17: When they were gathered together, Pilate said to them, Whom do you want me to release to you? Bar-Abbas, or Jesus who is called the Christ?
27.18: For Pilate knew that because of envy they had delivered him.
27.19: When the governor was sitting on his judgment seat, his wife sent to him and said to him, Have nothing to do with that righteous man; for today I have suffered a great deal in my dream because of him.
27.20: But the high priests and the elders urged the people to ask for Bar-Abbas, and to destroy Jesus.
27.21: And the governor answered and said to them, Which of these two do you want me to release to you? They said, Bar-Abbas.
27.22: Pilate said to them, What shall I then do with Jesus who is called the Christ? They all said, Let him be crucified.
27.23: Pilate said to them, What evil has he done? But they cried out the more and said, Let him be crucified.
27.24: Now when Pilate saw that he was gaining nothing, but that instead confusion was increasing, he took water and washed his hands before the people, and said, I am innocent of the blood of this righteous man; do as you please.
27.25: All the people then answered and said, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
27.26: Then he released to them Bar-Abbas, and had Jesus scourged with whips, and delivered to be crucified.
27.27: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the Praetorium, and the whole company gathered around him.
27.28: And they removed his clothes and put on him a scarlet robe.
27.29: And they wove a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and a reed in his right hand; and they knelt on their knees before him, and they were mocking him and saying, Hail, King of the Jews!
27.30: And they spat in his face, and took the reed and struck him on his head.
27.31: And when they had mocked him, they took off the robe from him and put on him his own clothes, and took him away to be crucified.
27.32: And as they were going out, they found a man of Cyrene, whose name was Simon, whom they compelled to carry his cross.
27.33: And they came to a place which is called Golgotha, which is interpreted The Skull.
27.34: And they gave him to drink vinegar mixed with gall; and he tasted it, but he would not drink.
27.35: And when they had crucified him, they divided his clothes by casting lots.
27.36: And they were sitting there and watching him.
27.37: And they placed above his head in writing the reason for his death: THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
27.38: And there were crucified with him two bandits, one on his right and one on his left.
27.39: And those who passed by blasphemed against him, nodding their heads,
27.40: And saying, O you who can tear down the temple and build it in three days, deliver yourself, if you are the Son of God, and come down from the cross.
27.41: The high priests likewise were mocking, together with the scribes, the elders and the Pharisees.
27.42: And they were saying, He saved others, but he cannot save himself. If he is the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, so that we may see and believe in him.
27.43: He trusted in God; let him save him now, if he is pleased with him; for he said, I am God’s Son.
27.44: The bandits also, who were crucified with him were reproaching him.
27.45: Now from the sixth hour, there was darkness over all the land, until the ninth hour.
27.46: And about the ninth hour, Jesus cried out with a loud voice and said, Eli, Eli, lmana shabachthani! which means, My God, my God, for this I was kept!
27.47: Some of the men who were standing by, when they heard it, said, This man has called for Elijah.
27.48: And immediately one of them ran and took a sponge and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.
27.49: But the rest said, Hush, let us see if Elijah will come to save him.
27.50: But Jesus again cried out with a loud voice, and gave up his breath.
27.51: And immediately the door curtains of the temple were rent in two, from the top to the bottom; and the earth quaked, and the rocks split;
27.52: And the tombs were opened; and the bodies of a great many saints who were sleeping in death rose up,
27.53: And they went out; and after his resurrection, they entered into the holy city, and appeared to a great many.
27.54: When the centurion and those who were with him watching Jesus, saw the earthquake and all that happened, they were very much frightened, and they said, Truly this man was the Son of God.
27.55: There were also many women there, who were looking from afar, those who had followed Jesus from Galilee, and who used to minister to him.
27.56: One of them was Mary of Magdala; and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
27.57: When evening came, there came a rich man of Arimathaea, whose name was Joseph, who was also a disciple of Jesus.
27.58: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. And Pilate commanded that the body should be given to him.
27.59: So Joseph took the body, and wrapped it in a shroud of fine linen,
27.60: And laid it in his own new tomb which was hewn in a rock; and they rolled a large stone, and placed it against the door of the tomb, and went away.
27.61: And there were there Mary of Magdala and the other Mary, who were sitting opposite the tomb.
27.62: The next day, which is after Friday, the high priests and the Pharisees together came to Pilate,
27.63: And they said to him, Our lord, we have just remembered that that deceiver used to say when he was alive, After three days I will rise again.
27.64: Now, therefore, command that precautions be taken at the tomb for three days. It is probable that his disciples may come and steal him at night, and then say to the people, He has risen from the dead; and the last deception will be worse than the first.
27.65: Pilate said to them, You have guards; go and take precautions as best you know.
27.66: So they went and kept a watch at the tomb, and together with the guards they sealed the stone.

28.1: IN the evening of the sabbath day, when the first day of the week began, there came Mary of Magdala and the other Mary, to see the tomb.
28.2: And behold, a great earthquake took place; for the angel of the Lord came down from heaven, and went up and rolled away the stone from the door, and sat on it.
28.3: His appearance was like lightning, and his garments white as snow.
28.4: And for fear of him the guards who were watching trembled, and became as if they were dead.
28.5: But the angel answered and said to the women, You need not be afraid; for I know that you are seeking Jesus who was crucified.
28.6: He is not here, for he has risen, just as he had said. Come in, see the place where our Lord was laid.
28.7: And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he has risen from the dead; and behold, he will be before you to Galilee; there you will see him; behold, I have told you.
28.8: And they went away hurriedly from the tomb with fear and with great joy, running to tell his disciples.
28.9: And behold, Jesus met them, and said to them, Peace be to you. And they came up and laid hold of his feet, and worshipped him.
28.10: Then Jesus said to them, Do not be afraid; but go and tell my brethren to go to Galilee, and there they shall see me.
28.11: When they were going, some of the guards came into the city, and told the high priests everything that had happened.
28.12: So they gathered with the elders and took counsel; and they gave money, not a small sum, to the guards,
28.13: Telling them, Say that his disciples came by night and stole him while we were sleeping.
28.14: And if this should be heard by the governor, we will appeal to him, and declare that you are blameless.
28.15: So they took the money, and did as they were instructed; and this word went out among the Jews, until this day.
28.16: The eleven disciples then went to Galilee to a mountain, where Jesus had promised to meet them.
28.17: And when they saw him, they worshipped him; but some of them were doubtful.
28.18: And Jesus came up and spoke with them, and said to them, All power in heaven and on earth has been given to me. Just as my Father has sent me I am also sending you.
28.19: Go, therefore, and convert all nations; and baptize them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit;
28.20: And teach them to obey everything that I have commanded you; and, behold, I am with you all the days, to the end of the world. Amen.
